report_name: Microsoft Warns of Social Engineering Campaign Abusing WhatsApp for Windows

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Social Engineering Actor
threat_actors.description: An unnamed threat actor conducting a social engineering campaign targeting WhatsApp for Windows users. The actor uses VBScript malware to install remote access software, relying on living-off-the-land techniques.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: The initial delivery mechanism is a malicious VBScript attachment sent via the WhatsApp messaging service, disguised as a legitimate document or image.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The attack's success is entirely dependent on the user being tricked into executing the initial malicious .vbs file.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Visual Basic
attack_patterns.description: The initial payload is a Visual Basic Script (.vbs) file executed by the Windows Script Host (wscript.exe).

attack_patterns.name: System Binary Proxy Execution
attack_patterns.description: The malware uses renamed, legitimate Windows binaries such as bitsadmin or certutil to download a second-stage payload from an attacker-controlled server, evading simple detection rules.

attack_patterns.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Bypass User Account Control
attack_patterns.description: The malware modifies User Account Control (UAC) settings, such as the EnableLUA registry value, to operate with fewer restrictions and suppress future warnings.

attack_patterns.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
attack_patterns.description: The malware creates registry entries in autostart locations, such as the Run key, to achieve persistence on the compromised machine.

malware.name: VBScript Downloader
malware.description: A malicious Visual Basic Script (.vbs) file delivered via WhatsApp. When executed, it uses living-off-the-land techniques to download and run a second-stage remote access payload.

malware.name: Remote Access Software
malware.description: The final payload installed by the VBScript downloader. It is delivered as an unsigned Microsoft Installer (MSI) package and gives the attacker full, persistent access to the victim's system.

tools.name: wscript.exe
tools.description: The Windows Script Host (wscript.exe) is used to execute the initial malicious .vbs file.

tools.name: bitsadmin
tools.description: The legitimate Windows BITSAdmin tool is copied, renamed, and used by the VBScript malware to download a second-stage payload from an attacker-controlled server.

tools.name: certutil
tools.description: The legitimate Windows certutil tool is copied, renamed, and potentially used by the VBScript malware to download a second-stage payload.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled Server
infrastructure.description: A remote server controlled by the attacker used to host and deliver the second-stage malware payload.

indicators.name: Suspicious VBScript Execution by WScript
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious VBScript Execution by WScript
id: 2b8f1c9d-5a3e-4b1f-8c9a-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of wscript.exe with a .vbs file argument, which is a common technique for initial execution in social engineering campaigns.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/04/whatsapp-on-windows-users-targeted-in-new-campaign-warns-microsoft
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
tags:
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wscript.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '.vbs'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of wscript.exe with a .vbs file, as seen in the WhatsApp social engineering campaign.

indicators.name: File Download via BITSAdmin
indicators.pattern:
title: File Download via BITSAdmin
id: 8c9a1d2e-3f4a-4b6c-9d2b-8f1c9d5a3e4b
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of bitsadmin.exe with the /transfer switch to download a remote file, a common living-off-the-land technique.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/04/whatsapp-on-windows-users-targeted-in-new-campaign-warns-microsoft
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
tags:
  - attack.t1218
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\bitsadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' /transfer '
      - ' /addfile '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of bitsadmin.exe to download payloads, a technique used in this campaign.

indicators.name: UAC Bypass via EnableLUA Registry Modification
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: UAC Bypass via EnableLUA Registry Modification
id: 5a3e4b1f-8c9a-4d2e-b8f1-c9d5a3e4b6c9
status: experimental
description: Monitors for modifications to the EnableLUA registry key, which can be used to disable User Account Control (UAC).
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/04/whatsapp-on-windows-users-targeted-in-new-campaign-warns-microsoft
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
tags:
  - attack.t1548.002
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: high
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|endswith: '\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\EnableLUA'
    Details: '0'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects attempts to disable UAC by modifying the EnableLUA registry value, a technique used by the malware in this campaign.

indicators.name: Persistence via Registry Run Key
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Persistence via Registry Run Key Creation
id: 1d2e3f4a-5b6c-4b8f-8c9a-1d2e3f4a5b6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a new value in the HKCU Run registry key, a common persistence technique.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/04/whatsapp-on-windows-users-targeted-in-new-campaign-warns-microsoft
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
tags:
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    EventType: 'SetValue'
    TargetObject|startswith: 'HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a new entry in the user's registry Run key for persistence, as used in this campaign.
